opportunities.title: BJA FY 25 Invited to Apply- Transforming Prison Cultures, Climates, and Spaces
opportunities.synopsis: This is a notice of funding opportunity (NOFO) for the BJA FY 2025 Invited to Apply for Transforming Prison Cultures, Climates, and Spaces. Originally released in FY23, BJA’s Transforming Prison Cultures, Climates, and Spaces program funded the development and initial implementation of the Safe Inside Initiative, which supports state departments of corrections (DOC) in their efforts to improve safety, security, and reduce recidivism, particularly among prisons facing acute workforce challenges. Through the Safe Inside Initiative funded via this program, state DOCs will receive highly specializ

opportunities.title: BJA FY25 Byrne State Crisis Intervention Formula Program
opportunities.synopsis: OJP is committed to advancing work that furthers DOJ’s mission to uphold the rule of law, to keep our country safe, and to protect civil rights. OJP provides federal leadership, funding, and other critical resources to directly support law enforcement, combat violent crime, protect American children, provide services to American crime victims, and address public safety challenges, including human trafficking and the opioid crisis. As authorized by the Bipartisan Safer Communities Act of 2022, this NOFO will provide funding for the creation or enhancement of state crisis intervention court proc

opportunities.title: Estimated Fiscal Year 2026 Adult Education and Family Literacy Act State Award Amounts
opportunities.synopsis: This program memorandum advises states of the estimated FY 2026 State Allocations under the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act (AEFLA), title II of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA).  Questions regarding this Program Memorandum may be emailed to Sabrina Guerrier (octae-formula@dol.gov).

opportunities.title: Fiscal Year 2026 State Allocations under the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 (Perkins V)
opportunities.synopsis: This program memorandum advises states of the estimated FY 2026 State Allocations under the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 (Perkins V).  Questions regarding this Program Memorandum may be emailed to Anu Mathew (octae-formula@dol.gov).

opportunities.title: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) Adult Activities Program Allotments for Program Year (PY) 2026.
opportunities.synopsis: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) Adult Activities Program Allotments for Program Year (PY) 2026.   States should e-mail all submission questions to Anu Mathew, Grants Management Specialist, at WIOA-WP-WIG-ETAGrants@dol.gov. The email must reference the specific Funding Opportunity Number, and include a contact name, email address, and phone number.

opportunities.title: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) Youth Program Allotments for Program Year (PY) 2026.
opportunities.synopsis: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) Youth Activities Program Allotment for Program Year (PY) 2026.  States should e-mail all submission questions to Anu Mathew, Grants Management Specialist, at WIOA-WP-WIG-ETAGrants@dol.gov. The email must reference the specific Funding Opportunity Number, and include a contact name, email address, and phone number.

opportunities.title: Updated National Dislocated Worker Grant Program Guidance and Application Information
opportunities.synopsis: If you have difficulty accessing the full announcement electronically, please contact Jenifer McEnery (DOL-ETA-DWG@dol.gov).

foundations.name: Gates Foundation Trust
foundations.pitch: Postsecondary education access with emphasis on underserved adult learners.

foundations.name: Ford Foundation
foundations.pitch: Multi-year reentry, justice reform, and human rights grantmaking.

foundations.name: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation
foundations.pitch: Health equity and SUD-adjacent programming for justice-involved populations.

foundations.name: John D & Catherine T Macarthur Foundation
foundations.pitch: Safety and Justice Challenge — jail-population reduction + reentry.

foundations.name: Open Society Institute
foundations.pitch: Criminal-justice reform and reentry support across U.S. programs.

foundations.name: Carnegie Corporation Of New York
foundations.pitch: Education, democracy, and international peace — adult and postsecondary focus.

foundations.name: Kresge Foundation
foundations.pitch: Human services + economic mobility funding including reentry workforce.

foundations.name: Ascendium Education Group Inc
foundations.pitch: Largest philanthropic funder of postsecondary education in prison in the U.S.

foundations.name: Annie E Casey Foundation
foundations.pitch: Juvenile justice reform and economic opportunity for returning citizens.

foundations.name: Public Welfare Foundation Inc
foundations.pitch: Criminal + juvenile justice reform focused on underserved communities.

foundations.name: W K Kellogg Foundation
foundations.pitch: Children, family, and community grantmaking including justice-involved youth.

foundations.name: Ben & Jerrys Foundation
foundations.pitch: Grassroots social-justice grantmaking including reentry + justice organizing.

saa._about: State Administering Agencies (SAAs) for Byrne JAG pass-through. Each state designates an SAA that sub-grants federal DOJ funds to local agencies. Contacts/URLs verified from public DOJ/BJA and state sources; 'verify' flag marks entries where Nucleos should confirm before publishing. Update cadence: manual, as SAA directories change.
